title: Monotone Rule-Aware GBDT
summary: Some pit-risk relationships are directionally constrained by physics or rules: more tyre age, more positive lap-time loss, more finish deficit, and remaining dry-compound debt should not usually reduce pit likelihood within the same regime.
rationale: Some pit-risk relationships are directionally constrained by physics or rules: more tyre age, more positive lap-time loss, more finish deficit, and remaining dry-compound debt should not usually reduce pit likelihood within the same regime. XGBoost/LightGBM monotone constraints are an adjacent competition pattern for reducing brittle overfit under shift.
implementation_hint: Create signed features such as `CurrentTyreFinishExcess`, `Positive_LapTime_Delta`, `TyreLife`, `StopDebtAfterCurrent`, and `TyreLife_To_Remaining`. Fit a constrained XGBoost or LightGBM specialist with monotonicity only on clearly signed features, leaving ambiguous regime features unconstrained; optionally split dry vs wet compounds first.
expected_effect: Lower variance and better public-score translation than an unconstrained tree on threshold-heavy engineered features.
risk: Some signs flip in wet races, SC/VSC periods, or mandatory-compound edge cases, so overly broad constraints can flatten real signal.
